lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Greetings human. Do you seek guidance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: I am Xorrah quo Sihar. I am one of the mystics of the Kahlith.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kahlith?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Yes. At one time we were like you, but then we met the Tasakaal. They taught us to have purpose. They taught us to serve the balance.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Our old lives were left behind. In their place, the Kahlith were born.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tasakaal?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Our teachers. They showed us the way and taught us to serve the balance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The balance?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: For every death, there must be life. For every life, there must be a death. This is the balance.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Our purpose, as taught to us by the Tasakaal, is to keep this balance, no matter the cost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A mystic?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Yes. All Kahlith serve the balance. To do this, we take on one of four roles.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: My role is that of a mystic. I learn from the Tasakaal and then pass on their teachings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: This is our home. In your tongue, it is known as Mount Karuulm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My tongue?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Yes. You speak the language of humans but we Kahlith have our own language.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: In the past, we have tried to teach our language to your kind. However, despite the simplicity of our tongue, success was limited.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: We find that it is easier to just use your language when your kind are around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give an example?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Of our language?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Well you'll find our language is quite a simple one. Our names are a good example of how it is used.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: I am Xorrah quo Sihar. The first word, 'Xorrah', is unique to me and it is how I can be identified.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: The second word, 'quo', is a basic Kahlith word that translates to 'the' in your language.

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: The final word, 'Sihar', is my title. There are four possible titles, one for each role in Kahlith society. My title translates to 'Mystic' in your tongue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So in my language your name would be Xorrah the Mystic?

lines.speaker: Xorrah quo Sihar
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm good thanks.
